ИИ, скорее всего, изменит работу и повысит производительность, но не приведет к радикальным экономическим или трудовым переменам, а также не заменит полностью человеческое принятие решений.
b08415052e62.original_description: Since ChatGPT’s debut, AI has been framed as everything from a world-changing breakthrough to an existential threat. But researchers like Princeton’s Arvind Narayanan argue the reality may be more incremental – a powerful technology that will reshape work and productivity over time, without overturning the basic limits of economics, labor, or human decision-making. Франшиза, вдохновленная японскими фильмами ужасов и такими произведениями, как "Чужие" и "Хэллоуин", успешно выжила и развивалась на протяжении трех десятилетий, продолжая пугать игроков.

6740d768dfcc.title: Twitter turned 20 and I feel nothing
6740d768dfcc.summary:
Twitter is officially 20 years old. In another reality, that might make me kind of nostalgic. I've been lurking and scrolling and tweeting for 16 years; most of my adult life. There was a time when Twitter was a place where some internet strangers became my IRL friends, when I was excited to "live-tweet". When my infinitely more well-adjusted friends would send me memes, I would smugly say "I saw that on Twitter days ago."

Twitter stopped being that place a long time

A groundbreaking new study may have once again upended our understanding of human prehistory in the Americas.

For years, the predominant theory of how humans arrived in the western hemisphere centred around the Clovis culture, which crossed the Beringia land bridge from Asia between 13,400 and 12,800 years ago, and spread south.

AI software that can be embedded into smart glasses has won a £1m prize for technology to help people with dementia.

Built into chunky, black-rimmed frames that have a camera, microphone and speakers, the tech – known as CrossSense – guides wearers through everyday life by means of a chatty assistant called Wispy.

Nasa has begun returning its towering SLS rocket and Orion spacecraft to its Florida launch pad before a planned flyby of the moon, after completing necessary repairs.

Artemis engineers began the manoeuvre, which can take up to 12 hours, at 8pm local time. The US space agency will then begin the final preparations before its next launch window opens on 1 April.

2ef599a73423.summary: В новом подкасте The Guardian обсуждаются три интересных научных исследования: связь физических упражнений с улучшением работы мозга, способность ежей воспринимать высокочастотный ультразвук и влияние предвзятых алгоритмов искусственного интеллекта на формирование убеждений пользователей.  Рассматриваются потенциальные последствия этих открытий для здоровья человека, сохранения природы и влияния технологий на мышление.

2a8201512a4c.summary:
Physicist who won a Nobel prize for his work on superfluids and superconductors at Sussex University in the 1970s

The launch in the 1950s of Sputnik, the world’s first artificial satellite, played an unexpected but important role in propelling Anthony Leggett towards his 2003 Nobel prize for physics. Leggett, who was to become a world-leading researcher in the field of low-temperature physics, had, in 1959, just graduated in greats, a combination of classical literature, ancient history

45cee68301de.title: This crocodile ran like a greyhound across prehistoric Britain 200 million years ago
45cee68301de.summary: A newly discovered Triassic reptile from the UK looked more like a racing greyhound than a crocodile, built for speed on land. With long legs and a lightweight body, it hunted small animals in a dry, upland environment millions of years ago. Scientists identified it as a new species after spotting key differences in its fossils. It’s also a tribute to an inspiring teacher who helped spark a future scientist’s curiosity.

7553ffdabe2b.title: New AI tool predicts cancer spread with surprising accuracy
7553ffdabe2b.summary: Researchers have discovered that cancer spread isn’t random—it follows a kind of biological “program.” By studying colon tumor cells, they identified gene patterns that signal whether a cancer is likely to metastasize. Their AI model, MangroveGS, can predict this risk with about 80% accuracy and even works across multiple cancer types. This could transform how doctors decide who needs aggressive treatment and who doesn’t.

67fc711b305b.title: Scientists just found a hidden 48-dimensional world in quantum light
67fc711b305b.summary: A routine quantum optics technique just revealed an extraordinary secret: entangled light can carry incredibly complex topological structures. Researchers found these hidden patterns reach up to 48 dimensions, offering a vast new “alphabet” for encoding quantum information. Unlike previous assumptions, this topology can emerge from a single property of light—orbital angular momentum.

fbe9ab22476f.title: Harvard engineers build chip that can twist and control light in real time
fbe9ab22476f.summary: Scientists at Harvard have built a miniature device that can twist and tune light in real time. By rotating two stacked photonic crystals and adjusting their spacing with a tiny mechanical system, they can control how light’s “handedness” behaves. This allows the chip to distinguish between left- and right-circular polarized light with remarkable precision. The advance could lead to smarter sensors, faster communications, and new quantum technologies.

21ff2349ee1c.title: New pill cuts “bad” cholesterol by 60% in major trial
21ff2349ee1c.summary: A new pill, enlicitide, reduced LDL (“bad”) cholesterol by about 60% in a large clinical trial, matching the power of injectable therapies. Because it’s taken orally, it could overcome one of the biggest barriers keeping patients from using current treatments. Researchers say many people still don’t reach safe cholesterol levels—even on statins—highlighting the need for better options.

8b20a1e32576.title: A massive freshwater reservoir is hiding under the Great Salt Lake
8b20a1e32576.summary: В результате аэромагнитных исследований обнаружена обширная система пресной воды под Великим соляным озером, простирающаяся на глубину до 4 километров.  Это открытие, начавшееся с необычных возвышенностей, образованных под давлением подземных вод, может иметь значение для контроля пылевых бурь, вызванных высыханием озера. Исследователи изучают потенциал этой подземной воды в решении экологических проблем.
8b20a1e32576.original_description: A hidden freshwater system deep beneath the Great Salt Lake has been revealed using airborne electromagnetic surveys. Scientists found that freshwater extends much farther under the lake than expected, reaching depths of up to 4 kilometers. The discovery began with mysterious reed-covered mounds formed by pressurized groundwater pushing upward. Researchers are now investigating whether this underground water could help control hazardous dust from the drying lakebed.

70cf19487f1e.title: Hidden antibiotics in river fish spark new food safety fears
70cf19487f1e.summary: Antibiotics are accumulating in a major Brazilian river, especially during the dry season when pollution becomes more concentrated. Scientists even detected a banned drug inside fish sold for food, raising concerns about human exposure. A common aquatic plant showed promise in removing these chemicals from water—but it also altered how fish absorb them, creating unexpected risks.

1ae83686ee9e.title: Tectonic shift: Earth was already moving 3.5 billion years ago
1ae83686ee9e.summary: Scientists have uncovered the oldest direct evidence yet that Earth’s tectonic plates were on the move 3.5 billion years ago. By analyzing magnetic fingerprints in ancient rocks, they reconstructed how parts of the planet slowly drifted and even rotated over time. This challenges long-standing ideas that early Earth may have had a rigid, unmoving surface. Instead, it suggests the planet was already dynamic—and possibly setting the stage for life—much earlier than expected.

f99c71da7eac.title: Scientists turn probiotic bacteria into tumor-hunting cancer killers
f99c71da7eac.summary: Scientists have engineered probiotic bacteria to act as tumor-seeking drug factories. In mice, these bacteria infiltrated tumors and produced a cancer-fighting drug right where it was needed. This targeted approach could make treatments more effective and reduce side effects. More research is needed before it can be tested in people.

41a4d40399e6.title: These “forever chemicals” could be weakening kids’ bones for life
41a4d40399e6.summary: “Forever chemicals” may be affecting kids in ways that last a lifetime. A new study links early PFAS exposure to lower bone density during the teen years, especially in girls. Researchers also found that when exposure happens may play a key role. Reducing exposure during childhood could help protect long-term bone health.

6a09342909eb.title: Closing your eyes to hear better might be a big mistake
6a09342909eb.summary: Many people believe closing their eyes sharpens hearing, but that is not always true. In noisy settings, participants struggled more to hear faint sounds with their eyes closed, while matching visuals made it easier. Researchers found that shutting the eyes leads the brain to over-filter incoming sounds. Keeping your eyes open may actually improve how well you hear in noise.

a81e3fe2c392.title: Ultra-processed foods linked to 67% higher risk of heart attack and stroke
a81e3fe2c392.summary: Новое исследование в США показало, что употребление большого количества ультраобработанных продуктов, таких как чипсы и полуфабрикаты, связано с повышенным риском сердечных приступов и инсультов. У людей, потребляющих около девяти порций в день, риск этих заболеваний увеличился на 67% по сравнению с теми, кто ест около одной порции. Даже небольшое увеличение ежедневного потребления ультраобработанных продуктов связано с ростом риска сердечно-сосудистых событий.
a81e3fe2c392.original_description: Eating large amounts of ultra-processed foods like chips, frozen meals, sugary drinks, and packaged snacks may significantly raise the risk of serious heart problems. In a major U.S. study, people consuming around nine servings per day had a 67% higher risk of heart attacks, strokes, or death from heart disease compared to those eating about one serving. The risk didn’t just jump at high levels either. Each additional daily serving increased the likelihood of these events by more than 5%, even a

89b96ecc27a1.title: Belly fat linked to heart failure risk even in people with normal weight
89b96ecc27a1.summary: Carrying extra fat around the waist may be more dangerous than the number on the scale suggests. Researchers found that belly fat was more strongly linked to heart failure risk than BMI, even in people with normal weight. Inflammation seems to play a key role, helping explain why this type of fat is especially harmful. Measuring waist size could offer a simple way to detect hidden risk earlier.

7f90a42d33d7.title: The best strength training plan might be simpler than you think
7f90a42d33d7.summary: New strength training guidelines emphasize that doing any resistance training is what truly matters. Based on decades of research, experts say even simple routines can increase muscle, strength, and physical function. The key is not perfection but consistency. In fact, the best workout plan is the one you can maintain long term.

ad9f4c2056fd.title: Gum disease bacterium linked to breast cancer growth and spread
ad9f4c2056fd.summary: A common oral bacterium tied to gum disease may help spark and fuel breast cancer, according to new research. Scientists discovered it can travel through the bloodstream to breast tissue, where it causes DNA damage and speeds tumor growth and spread. It also appears to make cancer cells more aggressive and resistant to therapy. The effect is even stronger in people with BRCA1 mutations, raising new questions about the role of oral health in cancer risk.

98e4ec298311.title: Scientists solve 12,800-year-old climate mystery hidden in Greenland ice
98e4ec298311.summary: A mysterious spike of platinum buried deep in Greenland’s ice has long fueled theories of a catastrophic comet or asteroid strike 12,800 years ago—possibly triggering a sudden return to icy conditions known as the Younger Dryas. But new research points to a far less dramatic, yet still powerful culprit: volcanic eruptions. Scientists found the platinum signal doesn’t match space debris and actually appeared decades after the cooling began, ruling out an impact as the trigger.

fcb3e74559ec.title: Men are losing a key chromosome with age and it may be deadly
fcb3e74559ec.summary: Aging men often lose the Y chromosome in a growing number of their cells—and it may be far more dangerous than once believed. This loss has been linked to heart disease, cancer, Alzheimer’s, and shorter lifespans. Researchers suspect Y-less cells may grow faster and disrupt normal body functions. What seemed like a minor genetic quirk could actually be a major driver of age-related disease.

b92cc36b057f.title: This virus therapy supercharges the immune system against brain cancer
b92cc36b057f.summary: Ученые разработали новый метод терапии, использующий модифицированный вирус для усиления иммунного ответа против глиобластомы – агрессивного типа опухоли головного мозга. Инъекция вируса позволяет ему проникать в опухоль, уничтожать раковые клетки и активировать иммунные клетки, что, по предварительным данным, способствует увеличению продолжительности жизни пациентов.
b92cc36b057f.original_description: Scientists have found a way to make one of the most aggressive brain tumors vulnerable to the immune system. A single injection of a modified virus can invade glioblastoma, kill cancer cells, and summon immune fighters deep into the tumor. These immune cells persist and attack, which was linked to longer survival in patients.

104384f22bda.title: Scientists turn CO2 into fuel using breakthrough single-atom catalyst
104384f22bda.summary: Ученые разработали новый одноатомный катализатор, который эффективно превращает CO2 в метанол.  В отличие от традиционных катализаторов, использующих кластеры металлов, этот использует отдельные атомы индия, что снижает потребление энергии и упрощает оптимизацию процесса.  Это достижение может способствовать переходу к более экологичным источникам топлива и устойчивому химическому производству.
104384f22bda.original_description: Researchers have created a cutting-edge catalyst that turns CO2 into methanol more efficiently than ever before. Instead of using clumps of metal atoms, they engineered a system where each single indium atom actively drives the reaction. This dramatically reduces energy needs while making the process easier to study and optimize. The result could accelerate the shift toward cleaner fuels and sustainable chemical production.

cd9e0259abc0.title: This common vaccine cuts heart risk nearly in half in new study
cd9e0259abc0.summary: Новое исследование показало, что вакцина против опоясывающего герпеса может значительно снизить риск сердечных заболеваний. У людей с высоким риском вакцина снизила серьезные сердечные события на 46% и смертность на 66% в течение года, предположительно, за счет предотвращения осложнений, связанных с тромбами.
cd9e0259abc0.original_description: A shingles vaccine might double as a powerful heart protector. In people already at high risk, it cut major cardiac events by 46% and deaths by an impressive 66% within a year. Scientists think preventing shingles may also stop clot-related complications that can lead to heart attacks and strokes. The effect is so strong, it rivals the benefits of quitting smoking.

Эти миграции, ранее практически незамеченные, становятся особенно важными для изучения в связи с резким сокращением численности насекомых.  Ученые используют современные методы для отслеживания их перемещений, опираясь на исторические наблюдения, например, за массовыми миграциями в Пиренеях в 1950 году.

0b05f805aee6.original_description:
A Princeton University Art Museum show captures the urgent power of the iconic abstract expressionist’s 1945-50 breakthrough years

Artist Willem de Kooning held his first solo exhibition at Charles Egan Gallery in spring 1948, just shy of his 44th birthday. A smash success, the exhibition made his reputation, repositioning the artist and catapulting him to international renown by the early 1950s. By the decade’s end, many would consider him the world’s pre-eminent painter.

Награду вручил генеральный директор украинских железных дорог в знак признательности за визит актера в страну и его участие в освещении событий. 549225065d05.original_description:
The actor skipped Academy Awards ceremony to travel to Ukraine, where he was presented with alternative prize

Sean Penn has been presented with an Oscar fashioned from the metal of a Ukrainian railway carriage damaged by Russian missiles.

The statue, which is flat, silver and shaped like an Academy Award, was given to him by Oleksandr Pertsovskyi, CEO of Ukrainian railways, who told him: “You’re missing Oscars, so we made this one.”

Несмотря на потенциал, сериал не оправдывает ожиданий, но может предложить приятное восьмичасовое развлечение, если снизить планку.  Сериал с самого начала задает тон, показывая трех женщин, наслаждающихся жизнью, что создает впечатление более легкой драмы, чем напряженного триллера.
7876cb16a471.original_description:

d1b3d7610624.title: James breaks NBA appearance record as Lakers win thriller
d1b3d7610624.summary: LeBron James broke the NBA record for most regular-season games played on Saturday, taking the court for his 1,612th in the Los Angeles Lakers last-gasp 105-104 victory against the Orlando Magic.James, playing his unprecedented 23rd season, already holds a host of NBA records, including points scored, field goals made and field goals attempted and the longest streak of regular-season games with at least 10 points scored -- 1,297 -- in history.

22911969d51f.title: Cole Caufield has a hat trick in 5-point game to help the Canadiens beat the Islanders 7-3
22911969d51f.summary: Cole Caufield scored three goals to push his season total to 43 and added two assists to help the Montreal Canadiens beat the New York Islanders 7-3 on Saturday night.  Caufield is second in the NHL in goals, two behind Colorado star Nathan MacKinnon.  The Canadiens entered the day third in the Atlantic Division, while the Islanders were the first team outside the playoffs in the tight Eastern Conference.
